title: QUIC Hosted Interop-Runner Supported-Subset Coverage Verification
verification_method: Workflow review, requirement-home tests, local helper dry-run plans, hosted workflow dispatch, artifact inspection, SpecTrace validation, and Workbench validation.
preconditions: The repo-local helper continues to own image replacement, runner invocation, artifact capture, and advisory classification under `REQ-QUIC-INT-0013`.
Hosted dispatch requires GitHub Actions access, a current external `quic-interop-runner` checkout, Docker Engine 28.1 or later, Python 3.12, and Wireshark packet-analysis tools.
scope: Verify that the hosted workflow exposes an opt-in `supported-subset` coverage profile for already-supported helper cells while preserving the default hosted-handshake profile, manual-only trigger, per-cell artifact preservation, and advisory boundary. This verification records one hosted supported-subset dispatch as advisory evidence and does not prove broad interop readiness.
procedure: Review `.github/workflows/interop-runner-handshake.yml` and confirm it is `workflow_dispatch` only.
Confirm the workflow exposes a `coverage_profile` choice input whose default is `hosted-handshake` and whose wider option is `supported-subset`.
Confirm the workflow includes the narrow server-role handshake cell plus supported-subset matrix cells for retry, transfer, and multiconnect using the existing helper arguments.

Confirm the workflow invokes `scripts/interop/Invoke-QuicInteropRunner.ps1` with matrix-provided `LocalRole`, `ImplementationSlot`, `PeerImplementationSlots`, and `TestCases` values plus an explicit `RunnerRoot` path.
Confirm each cell writes to a cell-specific artifact root and uploads that tree with `if: always()`.
Run the requirement-home tests for `REQ-QUIC-INT-0017` and the focused interop helper script tests.
Run dry-run helper plans for the hosted-handshake shape and supported-subset shape.
Dispatch the hosted supported-subset profile on the feature branch and inspect each uploaded `runner-report.json` plus selected output logs for exact cell results.
Run SpecTrace core validation and Workbench core validation.
expected_result: The repository configuration provides a manual default handshake profile and an opt-in supported-subset coverage profile that collect hosted artifacts for already-supported helper cells without changing ordinary CI gates, helper testcase support, public API support, runner registry participation, or broad interop-readiness claims.
status_summary: Passed for the repository configuration slice on 2026-04-30. Focused requirement-home and helper tests passed locally, dry-run helper plans resolved for the hosted-handshake and supported-subset shapes, and core SpecTrace plus Workbench validation stayed green. Hosted supported-subset run 25187885762 on commit 512a97764658bd3edab7a5b4113e72fcda908a0d completed successfully and the uploaded runner reports recorded `handshake=succeeded`, client `transfer=succeeded`, server `transfer=succeeded`, both-role `retry=succeeded`, and client-role `handshakeloss=succeeded`. This is advisory supported-subset evidence, not a broad readiness claim.

supplemental_sections.heading: Deferred Coverage
supplemental_sections.content: External-runner `post-handshake-stream`, public 0-RTT, public key update, migration controls, runner registry participation, and broad peer matrices remain outside this verification.
